---
title: "CI split search"
output: html_document
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r development, include=FALSE}
library(testthat)
```

<!--
 Source of truth for one fusen feature area. Run the inflate chunk at the bottom after editing.
-->

```{r development-load}
# Load already included functions if relevant
pkgload::load_all(export_all = TRUE)
```

# Split search

```{r, function-best_numeric_split}
#' Find the best numeric split by concentration-index gain
#'
#' Evaluate all admissible cutpoints of a numeric predictor and return the
#' split with the largest weighted concentration-index gain.
#'
#' Candidate cutpoints are formed at midpoints between distinct sorted values of
#' `x`. Each candidate is filtered using `ctrl$minbucket` and `ctrl$minprob`
#' before being scored with [weighted_ci_gain()].
#'
#' @param x A numeric predictor vector.
#' @param y A two-column numeric matrix whose first column is the socioeconomic
#'   ranking variable and whose second column is the health outcome.
#' @param wt A numeric vector of case weights with the same length as `x`.
#' @param varid Integer identifier of the splitting variable, passed to
#'   [partykit::partysplit()].
#' @param ctrl A list-like control object containing `minbucket` and `minprob`.
#' @param ci_fun A concentration-index scoring function, typically created by
#'   [ci_factory()].
#' @param return Either `"split"` to return only a [partykit::partysplit()]
#'   object, or `"candidate"` to also return the gain and left-child routing
#'   needed by the greedy tree builder.
#'
#' @return A `partysplit` object describing the best admissible binary split, or
#'   `NULL` if no split is available because all `x` values are tied or all
#'   candidate splits violate the control constraints.
#'
#' @export
best_numeric_split <- function(
    x,
    y,
    wt,
    varid,
    ctrl,
    ci_fun,
    return = c("split", "candidate")) {
  return <- match.arg(return)
  ord <- order(x)
  x <- x[ord]
  y <- y[ord, , drop = FALSE]
  wt <- wt[ord]

  cut_pos <- which(diff(x) != 0)
  if (!length(cut_pos)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  gains <- rep(-Inf, length(cut_pos))
  ci_parent <- ci_fun(y, wt)
  cum_wt <- cumsum(wt)
  total_wt <- cum_wt[length(cum_wt)]
  row_id <- seq_along(x)

  for (k in seq_along(cut_pos)) {
    pos <- cut_pos[k]
    wl <- cum_wt[pos]
    wr <- total_wt - wl

    if (wl < ctrl$minbucket || wr < ctrl$minbucket) next
    if ((wl / total_wt) < ctrl$minprob ||
        (wr / total_wt) < ctrl$minprob) {
      next
    }

    left <- row_id <= pos
    gains[k] <- .weighted_ci_gain_from_parent(
      y = y,
      wt = wt,
      left = left,
      ci_fun = ci_fun,
      ci_parent = ci_parent
    )
  }

  if (!any(is.finite(gains))) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  best_k <- cut_pos[which.max(gains)]
  cut <- (x[best_k] + x[best_k + 1L]) / 2
  left <- row_id <= best_k
  left_original <- logical(length(left))
  left_original[ord] <- left

  sp <- partykit::partysplit(
    varid = as.integer(varid),
    breaks = cut,
    index = 1:2
  )

  if (identical(return, "split")) {
    return(sp)
  }

  list(
    gain = gains[which.max(gains)],
    varid = as.integer(varid),
    partysplit = sp,
    split = sp,
    left = left_original,
    type = "numeric",
    cutpoint = cut
  )
}
```

```{r, example-best_numeric_split}
x <- c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10)
y <- cbind(
  ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
  health = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1)
)
wt <- rep(1, 5)
ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

split <- best_numeric_split(
  x = x,
  y = y,
  wt = wt,
  varid = 1,
  ctrl = ctrl,
  ci_fun = ci_factory("CI")
)
split$breaks
```

```{r, tests-best_numeric_split}
test_that("best_numeric_split returns
 NULL when all x values are tied", {
  x <- c(5, 5, 5)
  y <- cbind(ses = c(10, 20, 30), health = c(1, 0, 1))
  wt <- rep(1, 3)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

  expect_null(best_numeric_split(x, y, wt, 1, ctrl, ci_factory("CI")))
})

test_that("best_numeric_split returns NULL
when no candidate split is admissible", {
  x <- c(1, 2, 3, 4)
  y <- cbind(ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40), health = c(1, 0, 1, 0))
  wt <- rep(1, 4)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 3, minprob = 0)

  expect_null(best_numeric_split(x, y, wt, 1, ctrl, ci_factory("CI")))
})

test_that("best_numeric_split chooses the cutpoint with the largest gain", {
  x <- c(4, 1, 3, 2)
  y <- cbind(
    ses = c(40, 10, 30, 20),
    health = c(10, 1, 3, 2)
  )
  wt <- rep(1, 4)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)^2

  split <- best_numeric_split(x, y, wt,
    varid = 7, ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = mock_ci_fun
  )

  expect_s3_class(split, "partysplit")
  expect_equal(split$varid, 7L)
  expect_equal(split$breaks, 3.5)
  expect_equal(split$index, 1:2)
})

test_that("best_numeric_split respects minprob when selecting splits", {
  x <- c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
  y <- cbind(
    ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
    health = c(1, 5, 10, 20, 40)
  )
  wt <- rep(1, 5)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0.4)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)^2

  split <- best_numeric_split(x, y, wt,
    varid = 1, ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = mock_ci_fun
  )

  expect_equal(split$breaks, 3.5)
})

test_that("best_numeric_split can return a reusable split candidate", {
  x <- c(4, 1, 3, 2)
  y <- cbind(
    ses = c(40, 10, 30, 20),
    health = c(10, 1, 3, 2)
  )
  wt <- rep(1, 4)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)^2

  candidate <- best_numeric_split(
    x = x,
    y = y,
    wt = wt,
    varid = 7,
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = mock_ci_fun,
    return = "candidate"
  )

  expect_type(candidate$gain, "double")
  expect_s3_class(candidate$partysplit, "partysplit")
  expect_equal(candidate$left, c(FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE))
})
```

```{r, function-best_factor_split}
#' Find the best factor split by concentration-index gain
#'
#' Evaluate cumulative binary splits of a factor predictor after ordering the
#' observed levels by weighted mean outcome, and return the split with the
#' largest weighted concentration-index gain.
#'
#' Only levels present among the kept observations are scored, but the returned
#' split keeps the full level mapping so it can be used with
#' [partykit::partysplit()].
#'
#' @param x_full A factor predictor containing all original levels.
#' @param keep A logical vector selecting the observations currently available
#'   for splitting.
#' @param y_full A two-column numeric matrix whose first column is the
#'   socioeconomic ranking variable and whose second column is the health
#'   outcome.
#' @param wt_full A numeric vector of case weights for all observations.
#' @param varid Integer identifier of the splitting variable, passed to
#'   [partykit::partysplit()].
#' @param ctrl A list-like control object containing `minbucket` and `minprob`.
#' @param ci_fun A concentration-index scoring function, typically created by
#'   [ci_factory()].
#' @param return Either `"split"` to return only a [partykit::partysplit()]
#'   object, or `"candidate"` to also return the gain and left-child routing
#'   needed by the greedy tree builder.
#'
#' @return A `partysplit` object describing the best admissible split of the
#'   observed factor levels, or `NULL` if fewer than two levels are present
#'   after subsetting or if all candidate splits violate the control
#'   constraints.
#' @export
best_factor_split <- function(
    x_full, keep, y_full, wt_full, varid, ctrl, ci_fun,
    return = c("split", "candidate")) {
  return <- match.arg(return)
  # keep full level mapping for partysplit()
  full_levels <- levels(x_full)
  x <- x_full[keep]
  y <- y_full[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  wt <- wt_full[keep]

  present_levels <- full_levels[full_levels %in% unique(as.character(x))]
  if (length(present_levels) <= 1L) return(NULL)

  ci_parent <- ci_fun(y, wt)

  # order factor levels by weighted mean outcome, then search cumulative
  # binary splits
  level_score <- sapply(
    present_levels,
    function(lv) {
      idx <- as.character(x) == lv
      stats::weighted.mean(y[idx, 2], wt[idx])
    }
  )

  ord_levels <- present_levels[order(level_score)]
  level_weight <- sapply(
    ord_levels,
    function(lv) {
      sum(wt[as.character(x) == lv], na.rm = TRUE)
    }
  )
  cum_level_weight <- cumsum(level_weight)
  total_weight <- cum_level_weight[length(cum_level_weight)]
  gains <- rep(-Inf, length(ord_levels) - 1L)

  for (k in seq_len(length(ord_levels) - 1L)) {
    wl <- cum_level_weight[k]
    wr <- total_weight - wl

    if (wl < ctrl$minbucket || wr < ctrl$minbucket) next
    if ((wl / total_weight) < ctrl$minprob ||
        (wr / total_weight) < ctrl$minprob) {
      next
    }

    left_levels <- ord_levels[seq_len(k)]
    left <- as.character(x) %in% left_levels
    gains[k] <- .weighted_ci_gain_from_parent(
      y = y,
      wt = wt,
      left = left,
      ci_fun = ci_fun,
      ci_parent = ci_parent
    )
  }

  if (!any(is.finite(gains))) return(NULL)

  best_k <- which.max(gains)
  left_levels <- ord_levels[seq_len(best_k)]
  right_levels <- ord_levels[-seq_len(best_k)]
  left <- as.character(x) %in% left_levels

  index <- rep(NA_integer_, length(full_levels))
  index[match(left_levels,  full_levels)] <- 1L
  index[match(right_levels, full_levels)] <- 2L

  sp <- partykit::partysplit(
    varid = as.integer(varid),
    index = index
  )

  if (identical(return, "split")) {
    return(sp)
  }

  list(
    gain = gains[best_k],
    varid = as.integer(varid),
    partysplit = sp,
    split = sp,
    left = left,
    type = "factor",
    left_levels = left_levels,
    right_levels = right_levels
  )
}

```

```{r, example-best_factor_split}
x_full <- factor(
  c("low", "mid", "high", "low", "mid", "high"),
  levels = c("low", "mid", "high", "unused")
)
keep <- rep(TRUE, 6)
y_full <- cbind(
  ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  health = c(1, 2, 5, 1, 2, 5)
)
wt_full <- rep(1, 6)
ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

split <- best_factor_split(
  x_full = x_full,
  keep = keep,
  y_full = y_full,
  wt_full = wt_full,
  varid = 1,
  ctrl = ctrl,
  ci_fun = ci_factory("CI")
)
split$index
```

```{r, tests-best_factor_split}
test_that(
  "best_factor_split returns NULL when fewer than two levels are present",
  {
    x_full <- factor(c("low", "low", "mid"), levels = c("low", "mid", "high"))
    keep <- c(TRUE, TRUE, FALSE)
    y_full <- cbind(ses = c(10, 20, 30), health = c(1, 2, 3))
    wt_full <- rep(1, 3)
    ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

    expect_null(best_factor_split(
      x_full, keep, y_full, wt_full, 1, ctrl, ci_factory("CI")
    ))
  }
)

test_that(
  "best_factor_split returns NULL when no admissible candidate split exists",
  {
    x_full <- factor(c("low", "mid", "high"), levels = c("low", "mid", "high"))
    keep <- rep(TRUE, 3)
    y_full <- cbind(ses = c(10, 20, 30), health = c(1, 2, 3))
    wt_full <- rep(1, 3)
    ctrl <- list(minbucket = 2, minprob = 0)

    expect_null(best_factor_split(
      x_full, keep, y_full, wt_full, 1, ctrl, ci_factory("CI")
    ))
  }
)

test_that("best_factor_split chooses the best cumulative level split", {
  x_full <- factor(
    c("high", "low", "mid"),
    levels = c("low", "mid", "high", "unused")
  )
  keep <- rep(TRUE, 3)
  y_full <- cbind(
    ses = c(30, 10, 20),
    health = c(10, 1, 2)
  )
  wt_full <- rep(1, 3)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)^2

  split <- best_factor_split(
    x_full = x_full,
    keep = keep,
    y_full = y_full,
    wt_full = wt_full,
    varid = 4,
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = mock_ci_fun
  )

  expect_s3_class(split, "partysplit")
  expect_equal(split$varid, 4L)
  expect_equal(split$index, c(1L, 1L, 2L, NA_integer_))
})

test_that("best_factor_split respects minprob when scoring candidates", {
  x_full <- factor(
    c("low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"),
    levels = c("low", "mid", "high")
  )
  keep <- rep(TRUE, 5)
  y_full <- cbind(
    ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
    health = c(1, 2, 2, 10, 10)
  )
  wt_full <- rep(1, 5)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0.4)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)^2

  split <- best_factor_split(
    x_full = x_full,
    keep = keep,
    y_full = y_full,
    wt_full = wt_full,
    varid = 1,
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = mock_ci_fun
  )

  expect_equal(split$index, c(1L, 1L, 2L))
})

test_that("best_factor_split can return a reusable split candidate", {
  x_full <- factor(
    c("high", "low", "mid"),
    levels = c("low", "mid", "high", "unused")
  )
  keep <- rep(TRUE, 3)
  y_full <- cbind(
    ses = c(30, 10, 20),
    health = c(10, 1, 2)
  )
  wt_full <- rep(1, 3)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)^2

  candidate <- best_factor_split(
    x_full = x_full,
    keep = keep,
    y_full = y_full,
    wt_full = wt_full,
    varid = 4,
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = mock_ci_fun,
    return = "candidate"
  )

  expect_type(candidate$gain, "double")
  expect_s3_class(candidate$partysplit, "partysplit")
  expect_equal(candidate$left, c(FALSE, TRUE, TRUE))
})
```
```{r, function-best_split_for_one_variable}
#' Find the best CI split for one variable
#'
#' @param x A predictor vector.
#' @param y A two-column numeric response matrix.
#' @param wt Case weights.
#' @param varid Integer variable id used by [partykit::partysplit()].
#' @param ctrl A control list created by [ci_tree_control()] or compatible.
#' @param ci_fun Concentration-index scoring function.
#'
#' @return A split candidate list containing the gain, `partysplit`, and left
#'   child membership, or `NULL` when no admissible split exists.
#'
#' @export
best_split_for_one_variable <- function(x, y, wt, varid, ctrl, ci_fun) {
  stopifnot(length(x) == nrow(y), length(wt) == nrow(y))
  ctrl <- .ci_tree_normalize_control(ctrl)

  keep <- stats::complete.cases(y) & !is.na(x) & !is.na(wt) & wt > 0
  if (sum(wt[keep], na.rm = TRUE) < ctrl$minsplit) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  candidate <- NULL

  if (is.numeric(x) || is.integer(x)) {
    candidate <- best_numeric_split(
      x = x[keep],
      y = y[keep, , drop = FALSE],
      wt = wt[keep],
      varid = varid,
      ctrl = ctrl,
      ci_fun = ci_fun,
      return = "candidate"
    )
  } else if (is.factor(x) || is.ordered(x)) {
    candidate <- best_factor_split(
      x_full = x,
      keep = keep,
      y_full = y,
      wt_full = wt,
      varid = varid,
      ctrl = ctrl,
      ci_fun = ci_fun,
      return = "candidate"
    )
  }

  if (is.null(candidate)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  left <- rep(FALSE, length(x))
  left[keep] <- candidate$left
  candidate$left <- left
  candidate
}
```

```{r, function-best_global_ci_split}
#' Find the globally best greedy concentration-index split
#'
#' Search across all supplied variables and their admissible split points, then
#' return the variable-split pair with the largest concentration-index gain.
#'
#' @param x A data frame or list of predictors.
#' @param y A two-column numeric response matrix.
#' @param wt Case weights.
#' @param ctrl A control list created by [ci_tree_control()] or compatible.
#' @param ci_fun Concentration-index scoring function.
#' @param vars Optional integer variable ids to search. Defaults to all columns
#'   of `x`.
#'
#' @return A split candidate list, or `NULL` when no variable has an admissible
#'   gain above `control$min_gain` and `control$min_relative_gain`.
#'
#' @export
best_global_ci_split <- function(x,
                                 y,
                                 wt,
                                 ctrl,
                                 ci_fun,
                                 vars = seq_along(x)) {
  stopifnot(is.matrix(y), nrow(y) == length(wt))
  ctrl <- .ci_tree_normalize_control(ctrl)

  vars <- as.integer(vars)
  vars <- vars[vars %in% seq_along(x)]

  if (!length(vars)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  if (!is.null(ctrl$mtry)) {
    vars <- sample(vars, min(ctrl$mtry, length(vars)))
  }

  parent_impurity <- ci_fun(y, wt)
  best <- NULL

  for (j in vars) {
    candidate <- best_split_for_one_variable(
      x = x[[j]],
      y = y,
      wt = wt,
      varid = j,
      ctrl = ctrl,
      ci_fun = ci_fun
    )

    if (!is.null(candidate) &&
        (is.null(best) || candidate$gain > best$gain)) {
      best <- candidate
    }
  }

  if (is.null(best) ||
      !is.finite(best$gain) ||
      best$gain <= ctrl$min_gain) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  best$parent_impurity <- parent_impurity
  best$relative_gain <- .ci_relative_gain(best$gain, parent_impurity)

  if (ctrl$min_relative_gain > 0 &&
      (!is.finite(best$relative_gain) ||
       best$relative_gain <= ctrl$min_relative_gain)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  best
}
```

```{r, tests-best_global_ci_split}
test_that("best_global_ci_split searches variables globally by CI gain", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
    outcome = c(1, 5, 10, 20, 40),
    weak = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1),
    strong = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
  )
  ctrl <- ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)^2

  split <- best_global_ci_split(
    x = toy_data,
    y = as.matrix(toy_data[c("rank", "outcome")]),
    wt = rep(1, nrow(toy_data)),
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = mock_ci_fun,
    vars = 3:4
  )

  expect_equal(split$varid, 4L)
  expect_s3_class(split$partysplit, "partysplit")
})

test_that("best_global_ci_split respects min_gain", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40),
    outcome = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
    x = c(1, 2, 3, 4)
  )
  ctrl <- ci_tree_control(
    minsplit = 1,
    minbucket = 1,
    minprob = 0,
    min_gain = Inf
  )

  split <- best_global_ci_split(
    x = toy_data,
    y = as.matrix(toy_data[c("rank", "outcome")]),
    wt = rep(1, nrow(toy_data)),
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = ci_factory("CI"),
    vars = 3
  )

  expect_null(split)
})

test_that("best_global_ci_split respects min_relative_gain", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(20, 10, 40, 30, 70, 50, 60),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 0, 1),
    x = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7)
  )
  y <- as.matrix(toy_data[c("rank", "outcome")])
  wt <- c(1, 3, 2, 4, 1, 2, 5)
  ci_fun <- ci_factory("CIg")

  ctrl <- ci_tree_control(
    minsplit = 1,
    minbucket = 1,
    minprob = 0,
    min_relative_gain = 0
  )

  split <- best_global_ci_split(
    x = toy_data,
    y = y,
    wt = wt,
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = ci_fun,
    vars = 3
  )

  expect_true(is.finite(split$relative_gain))
  expect_equal(split$relative_gain, split$gain / abs(ci_fun(y, wt)))

  ctrl$min_relative_gain <- split$relative_gain + 1e-8
  expect_null(best_global_ci_split(
    x = toy_data,
    y = y,
    wt = wt,
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = ci_fun,
    vars = 3
  ))
})
```

```{r development-inflate, eval=FALSE}
# Run but keep eval=FALSE to avoid infinite loop
# Execute in the console directly
fusen::inflate(
  flat_file = "dev/ci_split_search.Rmd",
  vignette_name = "CI split search"
)
```
